Title: A rice QTL GS3.1 regulates grain size through metabolic-flux distribution between flavonoid and lignin metabolons without affecting stress tolerance

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: HJX74; WYJ7 (9522) background used for validation; natural allele donor HP1 from Oryza glaberrima
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GS3.1 promoter (~2.5 kb upstream, complementation and GUS); rice ubiquitin promoter (constitutive, overexpression)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GS3.1 was cloned as the causal gene underlying the grain-size QTL GS3.1 and encodes a MATE transporter. 2. Loss of GS3.1 function enlarged grain length, width, and 1000-grain weight, while complementation or overexpression reduced grain size. 3. GS3.1 likely transports p-coumaric acid and redistributes metabolic flux between flavonoid and lignin/lignan branches in panicles. 4. Reduced GS3.1 activity lowered flavonoids and increased lignin-related metabolites, promoting cell expansion and yield without detectable stress-tolerance penalty in other tissues.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field growth under natural conditions in Shanghai and Hainan; panicle developmental stage was critical; no special external treatment required for main phenotype interpretation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed in panicles, leaves, culms and culm nodes, barely in roots; highest functional relevance in young panicles; in mature tissues enriched in phloem
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young panicles (3–5 cm), leaves, culms, culm nodes; minimal in roots; phloem-specific staining in mature leaves/culms; lower expression in NIL-GS3.1HP young panicles than NIL-GS3.1HJX
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Panicles/spikelet hull-associated developing grain tissues; lignin also assayed in leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids contribute to stress tolerance and suppress auxin transport/signaling; lignin contributes to cell wall formation, plant development and stress resistance
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane transporter with 12 transmembrane helices; likely endomembrane/late endosome-associated based on ortholog discussion
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MATE (multidrug and toxic compound extrusion) transporter family; 12-transmembrane domain protein
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics (LC-ESI-MS/MS); no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL fine mapping; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; genomic complementation; overexpression; GUS expression assay; qRT-PCR; E. coli ΔacrB growth complementation; structure prediction and molecular docking; metabolomics; lignin assay; split-ubiquitin assay; luciferase complementation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced GS3.1 activity increases grain size and yield, decreases flavonoid accumulation, and increases lignin/lignan-related metabolites by diverting p-coumaric acid away from flavonoid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Natural low-expression HP allele increased yield without detectable reduction in stress tolerance and without major changes in other tissues; overexpression reduced grain length and plant height
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The primary causal natural variation was identified from African rice donor HP1 (Oryza glaberrima), but the cloned rice locus corresponds to LOC_Os03g12790 in the cultivated rice genome. GS3.1 is not a catalytic enzyme or TF but a transporter controlling metabolic flux allocation. The HP allele contains coding polymorphisms and lower panicle expression. Paralogs Loc_Os04g48290 and Loc_Os06g36330 may provide redundancy outside panicles.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising breeding target for yield improvement by using the natural weak allele or panicle-specific downregulation/editing of GS3.1 to enlarge grain size while avoiding whole-plant flavonoid reduction and stress-sensitivity side effects
